summary: Excellent early‑era performance featuring a stellar Other One and beloved versions of He Was a Friend of Mine and Doing That Rag, captured on a well‑fixed audience recording.
review: Both reviewers rave about this early show, highlighting the powerful rendition of The Other One and their favorite takes of He Was a Friend of Mine and Doing That Rag. The tape has been “fixed” and praised as a great sounding source, making it enjoyable despite its likely audience origin. Overall, the musical flow and historic vibe earn high marks.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviews are 5‑star and emphasize strong performances and a good recording; limited sample size keeps confidence at medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer notes the tape has been ‘fixed’ and calls it an excellent early show recording; no specific soundboard comments but overall praised as great sounding.

standout_songs: He Was a Friend of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑era set with classic improvisational energy; the highlighted tracks showcase the band’s typical 1969 vibe.
